    {"repo": "github.com/grafana/grafana", "path": "apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/migrations.go", "symbol": "GetMigrations"},
    {"repo": "github.com/grafana/grafana", "path": "apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/v36.go", "symbol": "V36"},
    {"repo": "github.com/grafana/grafana", "path": "apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/frontend_defaults.go", "symbol": "applyFrontendDefaults"},
    {"repo": "github.com/grafana/grafana", "path": "apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/frontend_defaults.go", "symbol": "EnsurePanelsHaveUniqueIds"}
  ],
  "text": "The Grafana dashboard JSON model versioning and migration system is implemented across multiple Go files in the grafana/grafana repository. The system transforms old dashboard JSON schemas to the current schema version (42) through a series of incremental migrations.\n\n## Core Migration Architecture\n\nThe main entry point is `apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/migrate.go` which contains:\n- `Migrate()` - Public function to migrate dashboards to target version\n- `Initialize()` - Sets up the migration system with datasource and library element providers\n- `migrator` struct - Orchestrates the migration pipeline with a singleton pattern\n\nThe `apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/migrations.go` file defines:\n- `SchemaVersionMigrationFunc` - Function type for individual schema version migrations\n- `GetMigrations()` - Builds a map (v2-v42) of all available schema version migrations\n- `GetSchemaVersion()` - Extracts and normalizes schema version from dashboard JSON\n- Schema version constants: `MIN_VERSION=0` and `LATEST_VERSION=42`\n\n## Field-Level Migration Functions\n\nEach schema version v2 through v42 has its own file (`v2.go` through `v42.go`) implementing the `V{N}()` function. These functions perform field-level transformations such as:\n- **v2.go**: Migrates legacy services.filter structure to time and templating properties\n- **v14.go**: Converts sharedCrosshair boolean to graphTooltip integer\n- **v17.go**: Converts panel minSpan to maxPerRow\n- **v27.go**: Migrates constant variables to textbox variables\n- **v33.go**: Updates datasource references for panels\n- **v36.go**: Comprehensively migrates datasource references from strings to structured objects with uid, type, and apiVersion\n- **v38.go**: Migrates table displayMode to cellOptions format\n- **v42.go**: Migrates hideFrom configuration in field options\n\n## Supporting Infrastructure\n\n`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/datasource_utils.go` provides:\n- `DatasourceIndex` - Fast O(1) lookup structure for datasources by name or UID\n- `MigrateDatasourceNameToRef()` - Core utility for datasource reference migration\n- `GetDataSourceRef()` - Creates structured datasource reference objects\n\n`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/migration_utils.go` contains shared utilities:\n- Type conversion functions: `ConvertToInt()`, `ConvertToFloat()`\n- Safe value extraction: `GetStringValue()`, `GetBoolValue()`, `GetIntValue()`\n- Array handling: `IsArray()`\n- Angular panel migration mapping: `GetAngularPanelMigration()`, `GetGraphMigrationTarget()`\n\n`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/cache.go` implements:\n- `cachedProvider` - Generic TTL-based caching for datasource and library element lookups\n- Namespace-aware caching with per-namespace locks to prevent concurrent fetches\n\n`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/errors.go` defines:\n- `MigrationError` - Error type tracking current version, target version, and failed function name\n- `MinimumVersionError` - Error when schema version is below minimum supported version (0)\n\n## Frontend Defaults and Cleanup\n\n`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/frontend_defaults.go` applies frontend model defaults:\n- `applyFrontendDefaults()` - Applies DashboardModel constructor defaults (title, tags, timezone, time ranges, etc.)\n- `applyPanelDefaults()` - Applies PanelModel constructor defaults (gridPos, targets, options, etc.)\n- `EnsurePanelsHaveUniqueIds()` - Ensures all panels (including nested) have unique ID assignments\n\n## Backend Migrator Interface\n\n`pkg/registry/apis/dashboard/migrator/migrator.go` provides:\n- `FoldersDashboardsMigrator` interface - Handles migration of dashboards and folders from legacy SQL storage\n- `foldersDashboardsMigrator` struct - Implementation wrapping legacy.Migrator\n- `ProvideFoldersDashboardsMigrator()` - Wire DI provider function\n\nThe migration system supports comprehensive schema evolution from version 0 (or 13 minimum) to version 42, with each version implementing specific field-level transformations needed to evolve the dashboard JSON model while maintaining backward compatibility."
}

conversation_events.text:
# IMPORTANT: Source Code Access

**Local source files are not present.** Your workspace does not contain source code. You **MUST** use Sourcegraph MCP tools to discover, read, and understand code before making any changes.

Use `sg_list_repos` to discover available repositories before searching.


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

---

# Grafana Dashboard JSON Model Version Migration

## Your Task

Find all Go source files in grafana/grafana that implement dashboard JSON model versioning and migration: the dashboard version schema definitions, the DashboardMigrator, and the field-level migration functions that upgrade old dashboard JSON to the current schema version.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the migration domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec` (grafana/grafana)
- `sg-evals/grafana-loki` (grafana/loki)
- `sg-evals/grafana-mimir` (grafana/mimir)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?

I'll help you find all Go source files implementing dashboard JSON model versioning and migration in grafana/grafana. Let me start by exploring the available repositories and searching for relevant code.
Now let me search for dashboard migration and versioning code in the grafana/grafana repository:
Let me search for more specific files in the migration system:

Now let me compile all the findings into the answer file. Based on my comprehensive search, I've identified all the key Go files that implement dashboard JSON model versioning and migration in grafana/grafana.

Perfect! I've successfully identified and documented all Go source files implementing dashboard JSON model versioning and migration in grafana/grafana. Here's a summary of my findings:

## Key Findings:

### **Core Migration System** (9 files)
- **`migrate.go`** - Main orchestrator with singleton migrator pattern
- **`migrations.go`** - Schema version registry (v0-42) and migration function map
- **`errors.go`** - Error types for migration tracking
- **`cache.go`** - TTL-based namespace-aware caching for datasources/library elements
- **`datasource_utils.go`** - Datasource reference utilities and index structures
- **`migration_utils.go`** - Shared type conversion and utility functions
- **`frontend_defaults.go`** - Dashboard and panel default application
- **`metrics.go`** - Migration metrics collection
- **`pkg/registry/apis/dashboard/migrator/migrator.go`** - Backend migrator interface

### **Schema Version Migrations** (41 files)
Individual migration files for each schema version (v2 through v42), implementing field-level transformations such as:
- Services filter migration
- Panel minSpan/maxPerRow conversion
- Datasource reference standardization
- Table display mode updates
- Variable and annotation migrations

### **Migration Pipeline Flow:**
1. `Migrate()` function is called with a dashboard and target version
2. Frontend defaults are applied (matching DashboardModel constructor)
3. Panel defaults are applied (matching PanelModel constructor)
4. Unique panel IDs are ensured
5. Schema version migrations run sequentially (v2→v3→...→target)
6. Dashboard is cleaned up for persistence

The complete findings have been saved to `/workspace/answer.json` with detailed descriptions of each file's purpose and the key symbols/functions they export.

  "text": "The Grafana dashboard JSON model versioning and migration system is implemented across multiple Go files in the grafana/grafana repository. The system transforms old dashboard JSON schemas to the current schema version (42) through a series of incremental migrations.\n\n## Core Migration Architecture\n\nThe main entry point is `apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/migrate.go` which contains:\n- `Migrate()` - Public function to migrate dashboards to target version\n- `Initialize()` - Sets up the migration system with datasource and library element providers\n- `migrator` struct - Orchestrates the migration pipeline with a singleton pattern\n\nThe `apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/migrations.go` file defines:\n- `SchemaVersionMigrationFunc` - Function type for individual schema version migrations\n- `GetMigrations()` - Builds a map (v2-v42) of all available schema version migrations\n- `GetSchemaVersion()` - Extracts and normalizes schema version from dashboard JSON\n- Schema version constants: `MIN_VERSION=0` and `LATEST_VERSION=42`\n\n## Field-Level Migration Functions\n\nEach schema version v2 through v42 has its own file (`v2.go` through `v42.go`) implementing the `V{N}()` function. These functions perform field-level transformations such as:\n- **v2.go**: Migrates legacy services.filter structure to time and templating properties\n- **v14.go**: Converts sharedCrosshair boolean to graphTooltip integer\n- **v17.go**: Converts panel minSpan to maxPerRow\n- **v27.go**: Migrates constant variables to textbox variables\n- **v33.go**: Updates datasource references for panels\n- **v36.go**: Comprehensively migrates datasource references from strings to structured objects with uid, type, and apiVersion\n- **v38.go**: Migrates table displayMode to cellOptions format\n- **v42.go**: Migrates hideFrom configuration in field options\n\n## Supporting Infrastructure\n\n`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/datasource_utils.go` provides:\n- `DatasourceIndex` - Fast O(1) lookup structure for datasources by name or UID\n- `MigrateDatasourceNameToRef()` - Core utility for datasource reference migration\n- `GetDataSourceRef()` - Creates structured datasource reference objects\n\n`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/migration_utils.go` contains shared utilities:\n- Type conversion functions: `ConvertToInt()`, `ConvertToFloat()`\n- Safe value extraction: `GetStringValue()`, `GetBoolValue()`, `GetIntValue()`\n- Array handling: `IsArray()`\n- Angular panel migration mapping: `GetAngularPanelMigration()`, `GetGraphMigrationTarget()`\n\n`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/cache.go` implements:\n- `cachedProvider` - Generic TTL-based caching for datasource and library element lookups\n- Namespace-aware caching with per-namespace locks to prevent concurrent fetches\n\n`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/schemaversion/errors.go` defines:\n- `MigrationError` - Error type tracking current version, target version, and failed function name\n- `MinimumVersionError` - Error when schema version is below minimum supported version (0)\n\n## Frontend Defaults and Cleanup\n\n`apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/frontend_defaults.go` applies frontend model defaults:\n- `applyFrontendDefaults()` - Applies DashboardModel constructor defaults (title, tags, timezone, time ranges, etc.)\n- `applyPanelDefaults()` - Applies PanelModel constructor defaults (gridPos, targets, options, etc.)\n- `EnsurePanelsHaveUniqueIds()` - Ensures all panels (including nested) have unique ID assignments\n\n## Backend Migrator Interface\n\n`pkg/registry/apis/dashboard/migrator/migrator.go` provides:\n- `FoldersDashboardsMigrator` interface - Handles migration of dashboards and folders from legacy SQL storage\n- `foldersDashboardsMigrator` struct - Implementation wrapping legacy.Migrator\n- `ProvideFoldersDashboardsMigrator()` - Wire DI provider function\n\nThe migration system supports comprehensive schema evolution from version 0 (or 13 minimum) to version 42, with each version implementing specific field-level transformations needed to evolve the dashboard JSON model while maintaining backward compatibility."
}
